revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:犬養部]]
== Inukai-be ==
'''Inukai-be''' (犬養部) was a [[Shinabe clans|professional group]] (品部) during the pre-Taika era in Japan, serving the central government through their expertise in the breeding and use of {{ill|dogs|ja|犬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dogs|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イヌ|1=Dog|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q144}}.
== Origins and Significance of Inukai-be ==
The purposes of dog usage in Inukai-be have traditionally been explained in two ways:
1. For {{ill|Hunting|en|Hunting|qid=Q36963|lt=hunting|1=Hunting}} 2. For {{ill|guard duty|ja|守衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guard duty|1=guard|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q680928}}
According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', Inukai-be was established in August of the second year of Emperor Ankan's reign (538), following the mass establishment of {{ill|Miyake|ja|屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyake|1=Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11465761}} (imperial granaries) in May of the same year. Given the close timing of these events and the frequent proximity of place names like "Miyake" and "Inukai," it is widely believed that Inukai-be was tasked with guarding the Miyake using dogs.
While the exact founding period of Inukai-be remains uncertain, the presence of a place named "Inukai" at the site of Emperor Ankan's former residence, {{ill|Kagamihashi Palace|ja|勾金橋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagamihashi Palace|1=Magari no Kanahashi Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126889667}}, suggests its origins may date back to the first half of the 6th century. The frequent records of Miyake establishment in the ''Nihon Shoki'' from Emperor Ankan's era onward imply that the development of Miyake was closely linked to the establishment of Inukai-be. Moreover, the widespread expansion of Miyake is believed to have laid the groundwork for the later {{ill|provincial administrative system|ja|国・郡・里制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial administrative system|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Leaders of Inukai-be ==
Historical records such as the ''Nihon Shoki'' and the ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' mention several clans that led Inukai-be, known as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}:
* {{ill|Agata no Inukai no Muraji|ja|県犬養連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11582689}} * {{ill|Ama Inukai no Muraji|ja|海犬養連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Inukai no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579240|1=Q135579240}} * {{ill|Waka Inukai no Muraji|ja|若犬養連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waka Inukai no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579241|1=Q135579241}} * {{ill|Azumi Inukai no Muraji|ja|阿曇犬養連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi Inukai no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579242|1=Q135579242}} * Kara Inukai no Muraji (辛犬養連) * Ata Mite Inukai (阿多御手犬養)
While the title "Inukai no Muraji" without a prefix also appears in the ''Nihon Shoki'', it is likely due to missing clan names. Among these, Agata no Inukai, Ama Inukai, and Waka Inukai are considered prominent high-Ranking Tomo no Miyatsuko.
Some theories classify these clans as follows:
* Ama Inukai, Waka Inukai, Azumi Inukai, and Kara Inukai: associated with maritime clans (海神族系) * Ata Mite Inukai: related to the {{ill|Hayato people|en|Hayato people|qid=Q1151764|lt=Hayato people|1=Hayato people}} lineage * Agata no Inukai: linked to the {{ill|Yamatsumi clan|ja|山祇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Notably, the Agata no Inukai clan produced influential figures during the Asuka to mid-Nara periods, such as:
* {{ill|Agata no Inukai no Michiyo|en|Agata no Inukai no Michiyo|qid=Q11582685|lt=Agata no Inukai no Michiyo|1=Agata no Inukai no Michiyo}} – wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|qid=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} and mother of {{ill|Empress Kōmyō|en|Empress Kōmyō|qid=Q837932|lt=Fujiwara no Kōmyō|1=Empress Kōmyō}} and {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|qid=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}} * {{ill|Agata no Inukai no Hirotoji|ja|県犬養広刀自|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agata no Inukai no Hirotoji|1=Agatainukai no Hirotoji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11582687}} – mother of Prince Asaka, son of Emperor Shōmu
== Later History of Inukai-be ==
Initially focused on guarding Miyake, the Inukai-be eventually transitioned away from dog handling. They leveraged the martial skills honed through their security roles to evolve into a military clan. This shift is evidenced by the participation of Inukai clan members in the {{ill|Isshi incident|en|Isshi incident|qid=Q1062771|lt=Isshi Incident|1=Isshi incident}}—the coup that led to the assassination of {{ill|Soga no Iruka|en|Soga no Iruka|qid=Q736621|lt=Soga no Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}—including {{ill|Ama Inukai no Katsumaro|ja|海犬養勝麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Inukai no Katsumaro|1=Amanoinukai no Katsumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q51915273}} and {{ill|Katsuragi Waka Inukai no Amida|ja|葛城稚犬養網田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Waka Inukai no Amida|1=Katsuragi no Wakainukai no Amita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q51914871}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading ==
* Hiro Michi, ''Research on the Formation of the Ritsuryō State'' (律令国家成立史の研究) {{translated page|ja|犬養部|version=90573974|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:犬養部]]
